title: Fishing crew rescued near Gloucester after boat runs aground amid harsh seas
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two crew members were rescued after their fishing boat ran aground near Gloucester on Saturday amid harsh seas.
First responders included Gloucester Police, Fire Department, Harbormaster, and the US Coast Guard.
The crew used rescue lines to reach safety; both were uninjured despite significant damage to the boat.

title: The Rafael Devers trade is already haunting the Red Sox in 2026
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox have struggled since trading Rafael Devers to the Giants, starting the 2026 season with a 2-7 record.
Boston offloaded Rafael Devers' $254 million contract but hasn't seen return value from players acquired in the trade.
James Tibbs, traded away post-Devers deal, is excelling in Dodgers' Triple-A, highlighting Red Sox's asset mismanagement.

title: Why it might be time for a slumping Bruins team to call up James Hagens
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins have scored only three goals in their last three games, struggling with offensive performance.
19-year-old James Hagens, with 47 points in 34 games at Boston College, may offer a needed scoring boost.
Hagens currently plays for Providence Bruins on an AHL amateur tryout and could be signed to the NHL roster.

title: Tatum, Celtics pull away late to beat Raptors, move closer to 2-seed: 6 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: The Boston Celtics defeated the Toronto Raptors 113-95, with Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown leading the team.
Neemias Queta made significant contributions with 18 points, 7 rebounds, 4 assists, and 3 blocks, supporting Celtics' dominant paint performance.
Nikola Vucevic returned from a fractured finger injury, playing limited minutes and working to build chemistry ahead of the playoffs.

title: New Bedford man gets decades-long prison sentence for raping young relative
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Daniel Turcios Torres sentenced to 36-45 years for raping a young relative in New Bedford.
Victim reported years of abuse, including assaults by force and intimidation, to New Bedford police in 2024.
Turcios Torres faces an ICE detainer after serving his prison sentence; victim's courage praised by officials.

title: She is running Boston in honor of her mother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Madison Oakes, 23, from Boxford, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Massachusetts General Hospital.
Oakes is honoring her mother, who died of brain cancer after extended care from MGB cancer center.
A nurse in oncology, Oakes aims to help cancer patients and their families through her marathon run.

title: Boston police fatally shoot suspect allegedly wielding sword near Northeastern University campus
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police fatally shot a man allegedly wielding a sword during a mental health crisis near Northeastern University's campus.
The suspect stabbed a Boston officer and knocked an EMS clinician down before being shot, officials confirmed.
The incident follows recent violent occurrences in the area; authorities and the Suffolk County District Attorney are investigating.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her godmother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Abigail Dubois, 23, from Hudson, NH, runs the Boston Marathon to honor her late godmother who died of brain cancer.
Dubois supports the Cancer Care Equity program at Massachusetts General Hospital, aiding families with cancer-related expenses.
She qualified for the marathon in 2025 and is running with the Massachusetts General Hospital team in the 2026 race.

title: 1 dead, 2 hospitalized in fatal Brockton house fire, officials say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A woman in her 40s died in a house fire at 80 Glendale Ave. in Brockton Saturday morning.
Two people were hospitalized due to the fire, with intense flames mainly on the second floor.
Officials do not suspect arson; the fire started in a second-floor bedroom, cause still under investigation.

title: NH suspect who allegedly shot at family members killed in officer-involved shooting
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Masse, 38, was killed in a shootout with New Hampshire State Police in Raymond on Saturday night.
Masse allegedly wounded a Nottingham officer earlier that day after firing at family members with a long gun.
The officer involved shooting prompted a shelter-in-place order which was lifted after Masse was found and killed.
